places.name: Srikakulam — Kurma Temple
places.modernInfo: Only Kurma (tortoise) avatar temple in India. Vishnu took Kurma form and the temple sits on his cosmic form.

scenes.title: Vishnu becomes the cosmic tortoise
scenes.description: When gods and demons churned the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthan) using Mount Mandara as a churning rod, the mountain began to sink. Vishnu took the form of a massive tortoise and placed the mountain on his back — holding up the world so the churning could continue.

scenes.title: 14 treasures emerge from the ocean
scenes.description: From the churning emerged 14 divine treasures: Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth; Dhanvantari with amrita (nectar); the divine elephant Airavata; the wish-fulfilling tree Kalpavriksha; and the deadly poison Halahala that only Shiva could swallow.
